name: American Bank & Trust Company
description_short: Community bank operating in Louisiana since 1958, offering checking, savings, loans, and credit/debit cards with a focus on personal service.
description_long:
American Bank & Trust Company was founded on September 22, 1958, in Opelousas, Louisiana by Frank J. Diesi with a vision centered on honesty, integrity, loyalty, and trust. The bank has grown from its single Opelousas location to include branches in Krotz Springs, Port Barre, Lawtell, and Lafayette, serving individuals and businesses across south-central Louisiana. The institution has maintained its founding philosophy of providing personal attention and knowledgeable service for over 65 years.

The bank offers a comprehensive range of traditional banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), individual retirement accounts (IRAs), and various loan products. They provide American Bank VISA and MasterCard credit cards, debit cards with SecurLock fraud protection, and mobile and online banking platforms. Their loan services cover both personal and business lending needs, and they maintain current rate information accessible through their website.

American Bank & Trust Company distinguishes itself through its emphasis on community focus and long-term customer relationships. The bank highlights its commitment to personal service and having banking professionals genuinely interested in individual customer financial situations. Their longevity in the Louisiana market and multi-branch presence suggest established stability and local market knowledge.

As a regional community bank, American Bank & Trust Company is best suited for customers in south-central Louisiana seeking traditional banking services with personal relationship banking rather than digital-first or nationwide options. Their service area is geographically limited to their five branch locations, and they appear positioned as a conservative, relationship-focused institution rather than a technology-forward or full-service national bank. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established 1958 with 65+ years of operational history and local market presence
Multiple branch locations across south-central Louisiana (Opelousas, Krotz Springs, Port Barre, Lawtell, Lafayette)
SecurLock fraud protection on credit and debit cards
Wide range of products including checking, savings, CDs, IRAs, and loan services
Emphasis on personal service and relationship banking with knowledgeable staff
24/7 debit card support line (1-800-500-1044) for lost/stolen cards
Mobile and online banking platforms available (new platform coming May)
cons: Geographic limitation to Louisiana only—no national or interstate presence
Limited digital innovation—website appears dated with announcement of new online banking coming in May
No detailed rate information published on website; customers must inquire directly
Limited company size compared to regional or national banking alternatives
No mention of deposit insurance clarity or details on FDIC coverage
best_for: Small business owners in south-central Louisiana seeking community bank relationships
Individuals in Louisiana branch areas prioritizing in-person service and local decision-making
Customers seeking traditional banking products (checking, savings, CDs, basic loans) without online-only banking
Those who value long-term customer relationships over digital convenience
diagnosis: American Bank & Trust Company is best for individuals and small businesses in south-central Louisiana seeking traditional banking services with emphasis on personal relationships and local service. The primary caveat is geographic limitation—they operate only five branches in Louisiana, making them unsuitable for customers outside this region or those requiring digital-first banking experiences.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
